response.justification: Candidate has 14+ years of overall software experience, satisfying the seniority bar, but the role centers on C++ development for industrial automation and the resume shows only ~6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments back in 2014–2015. Primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with no evidence of MQTT, IEC 62443, industrial protocols, or message-broker-based large-scale C++ systems. Some adjacent strengths (event-driven systems, low-latency services in DLang, Node.js/React) exist, but the core C++ and industrial-cybersecurity requirements are not met.
strengths: 14+ years of senior-level engineering experience across distributed and event-driven systems
Familiarity with advantageous stack items: Node.js, React, Docker, Linux, CI/CD
Low-latency, high-throughput service experience (DLang at Sociomantic) shows performance mindset
concerns: Minimal and dated C++/STL experience (single ~6-month stint in 2014–2015)
No exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No evidence of MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IoT, or industrial automation domain work

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / MQTT background
Strong general senior engineering tenure and Node.js/React adjacency
